plan_metrics.plan_content:
Here's a summary of the plan I've written:

---

## Plan: `@prisma/rest-api-generator`

### What it does
A new Prisma generator package that implements the standard JSON-RPC generator protocol (`generatorHandler` from `@prisma/generator-helper`) and emits a fully typed TypeScript REST API client from any Prisma schema.

### Files to **create** (all new — `packages/rest-api-generator/`)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `package.json` | Package metadata, `bin` entry (`prisma-rest-api-generator → dist/generator.js`), deps on `@prisma/dmmf`, `@prisma/generator`, `@prisma/generator-helper` |
| `tsconfig.json` / `tsconfig.build.json` | Standard monorepo boilerplate |
| `helpers/build.ts` | `void build(unbundledConfig)` — same pattern as `client-common` |
| `src/type-mapping.ts` | DMMF scalar → TS type (`String→string`, `Int→number`, `DateTime→string`, etc.) |
| `src/model-interface.ts` | Generates 4 interfaces per model: `User`, `UserCreateInput`, `UserUpdateInput`, `UserWhereUniqueInput` |
| `src/crud-client.ts` | Generates a `UserClient` class with `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete` using native `fetch` |
| `src/rest-api-client.ts` | Generates top-level `RestApiClient` with one sub-client property per model |
| `src/generate-files.ts` | Orchestrates file writing; exports `onGenerate(options)` |
| `src/generator.ts` | CLI binary entry (`#!/usr/bin/env node`) — calls `generatorHandler` |
| `src/index.ts` | Library surface re-exports |

### Files to **modify**
- **`tsconfig.build.bundle.json`** — add `"@prisma/rest-api-generator": ["./packages/rest-api-generator/src"]` to `paths`
- **`.github/workflows/test-template.yml`** — add test step in `client-packages` job

### Generated output structure (per run of `prisma generate`)
```
generated/rest-client/
├── models/User.ts          ← 4 TypeScript interfaces
├── client/UserClient.ts    ← CRUD class (fetch-based)
├── RestApiClient.ts        ← Top-level client combining all models
└── index.ts                ← Barrel re-export
```
